message:
test(fuzz): add SchemaId::try_new ReservedSeparator error-field correctness fuzz (resolves 5lyvb)

SchemaId::try_new (lib.rs:74-98) rejects ':' or '@' in namespace/name
with SchemaIdError::ReservedSeparator { field, separator }. Existing
schema_id_hash tests injectivity + try_new accept/reject parity but
does NOT verify the error variant's field and separator values. A
regression that mis-labeled the field or returned a wrong separator
char would break operator diagnostics.

Six properties driven over arbitrary namespace/name strings:
- ':' in namespace → ReservedSeparator { field: "namespace", separator: ':' }
- '@' in namespace → "namespace", '@'
- ':' in name → "name", ':'
- '@' in name → "name", '@'
- namespace-first ordering: when both fields contain reserved chars,
  namespace error reports first
- clean inputs accept with the original namespace/name verbatim

Once-gated anchors verify each (field, separator) pair + namespace-
first ordering anchor exercises the lib.rs:81-82 reject_reserved call
sequence + clean acceptance counterpart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -822,5 +822,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_schema_id_try_new_error"
+path = "fuzz_targets/schema_id_try_new_error.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
